profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — Packet.decode is called within pyav_iter_frames
profile_overlap_rules.reason: Packet.decode cumulative is a subset of pyav_iter_frames cumulative; adding would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — to_ndarray is called within pyav_iter_frames for sampled frames
profile_overlap_rules.reason: to_ndarray cumulative is a subset of pyav_iter_frames cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — compute_histogram is called within extract_features
profile_overlap_rules.reason: compute_histogram cumulative is a subset of extract_features cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — cv2_calc_hist is called within compute_histogram for each channel
profile_overlap_rules.reason: cv2_calc_hist cumulative is a subset of compute_histogram cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — numpy histogram is the inner implementation of cv2_calc_hist
profile_overlap_rules.reason: numpy histogram cumulative is a subset of cv2_calc_hist cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — cv2_to_gray is called within extract_features
profile_overlap_rules.reason: cv2_to_gray cumulative is a subset of extract_features cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: serial_synchronous — all execute sequentially in the same thread within the for timestamp, image in frames loop
profile_overlap_rules.reason: These are all serial stages; Packet.decode and extract_features do NOT execute concurrently. The for loop advances only when the body completes.

pipeline_execution_model.description: pyav_iter_frames is a Python generator. detect_changes consumes it in a synchronous for loop. Each iteration: (1) the generator yields the next sampled VideoFrame via builtins.next, (2) ROI + extract_features + visual_distance + threshold execute in the same thread, (3) the loop body completes before the next generator advance. All processing is single-threaded: no worker pool, no queue, no concurrent decode/extract. Two passes both execute this same pattern sequentially.

candidate_evaluation.wall_clock_evidence: 92.61781430006886s (33.98%) — second-largest non-overlapping timer. Measured independently from decode; no decode overlap.
candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: 92.712s cumulative, 0.039s self, 1285 calls. Subsystem: compute_histogram 48.842s cumulative (40.567s numpy histogram), cv2_to_gray 34.049s cumulative.
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: slide_detector.detect_changes, measure('extract_features'), synchronous call to frame_features.extract_features on ROI-cropped image
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Largest single measured timer in the previous cycle (38.36% of total). Well-characterized profile with known sub-components (histogram ~49s, gray ~34s, phash+dhash ~7s). Called on every sampled frame (1201 Pass 1 + 84 dedupe).
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: Total decode across both passes (pass1_decode_or_frame_advance 96.36s + pass2_decode_or_frame_advance 76.09s = 172.46s, 63.27%) now directly measured and exceeds extract_features by 79.83s (29.29%). The gap is directionally stable across all three runs (31.27s to 79.83s). Individual single-pass decode (96.36s, 35.35%) also exceeds extract_features by 3.74s in the median run, though the ordering flips in run-02 where extract_features (136.39s) exceeds pass1_decode (114.76s).
candidate_evaluation.ruling: POSSIBLE_PRIMARY — second-largest component (33.98%) but SAFELY DISCRIMINATED as NOT the primary bottleneck when total decode pipeline cost is considered

candidate_evaluation.wall_clock_evidence: Directly measured: pass1_decode_or_frame_advance 96.363s (35.35%) + pass2_decode_or_frame_advance 76.094s (27.92%) = 172.457s (63.27%). Total decode exceeds extract_features by 79.83s (29.29%). Individual pass1_decode (35.35%) is the largest single measured stage.
candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: Packet.decode 114.854s self/cumulative (profile run). pyav_iter_frames 135.128s cumulative, 10.469s self. to_ndarray 9.58s self (2402 calls = 1201 sampled per pass).
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: pyav_backend.pyav_iter_frames — Packet.decode loop + sampling + to_ndarray for selected frames. Called from InstrumentedIterator which wraps builtins.next and accumulates wall-clock for the full generator advancement across both Pass 1 and Pass 2.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Total decode (172.46s) is the dominant wall-clock cost at 63.27% of detect elapsed — nearly double the second-largest component (extract_features at 92.62s). The gap (79.83s, 29.29%) is directionally stable across all three runs. Pass 1 decode alone (96.36s) is the largest single measured stage. Profile confirms Packet.decode (114.85s self) as the largest single function. F-0097 gap is resolved: residual dropped from 87.39s (33.0%) to 0.53s (0.19%), confirming all decode work is now captured by non-overlapping timers.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: The decode pipeline cost is split across two passes. Each individual pass's decode time is comparable to extract_features (pass1_decode 96.36s vs extract_features 92.62s — gap 3.74s, 1.4%). However, the decode pipeline is the SAME code running twice (once per pass), and its TOTAL cost dominates. An optimization targeting decode would reduce cost in both passes proportionally.
candidate_evaluation.ruling: PRIMARY — total decoder pipeline wall-clock (172.46s, 63.27%) SAFELY DISCRIMINATED as the dominant bottleneck. Evidence: (1) residual near zero confirming full attribution, (2) gap directionally stable across all runs, (3) profile corroboration of Packet.decode as largest single function.

candidate_evaluation.wall_clock_evidence: 0.04362679996120278s (0.016%) — independently measured via pass2_match_and_collect timer. The old pass2_collect timer (65.22s, 24.6%) was a MIXED timer; the newly split pass2_decode_or_frame_advance (76.09s, 27.92%) captures the decode portion, and pass2_match_and_collect (0.04s, 0.02%) captures only the collection overhead.
candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: Not visible in top profile entries — negligible.
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: Pass 2 for loop body: segment scanning, representative_timestamp matching, SlideRegion.process for matched frames, rep_frames insertion
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Pure collection overhead is now independently measured at 0.04s (0.02%). The previous mixed timer (pass2_collect) was correctly identified as entangled; the split confirms the decode portion dominates that timer.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: Trivially not a bottleneck at 0.02% of total wall-clock.

candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: Not visible in top profile entries — negligible.

candidate_evaluation.wall_clock_evidence: Residual 0.529s (0.19%) — down from 87.39s (33.0%). The F-0097 gap is RESOLVED. No significant hidden work remains. All non-overlapping timers account for 99.81% of detect elapsed time.
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: residual = detect_elapsed - measured_stage_total. Contains container setup, metadata parsing, segment building, service/repository overhead, document construction, persistence, timer overhead.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Honest assessment: the residual has fallen from 87.39s to 0.53s, confirming the instrumentation successfully captured the formerly hidden decode work.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: N/A — this was the fallback when specific discrimination failed. With F-0097 resolved, no longer needed.
candidate_evaluation.ruling: NOT_PRIMARY — residual is 0.19%; specific discrimination now succeeds

confidence_rationale: Total decode pipeline wall-clock (172.46s, 63.27%) dominates all other work by a factor of ~1.86x over the next largest component (extract_features, 92.62s, 33.98%). The gap (79.83s, 29.29%) is directionally stable across all three runs. Residual has dropped from 87.39s (33.0%) to 0.53s (0.19%), confirming full attribution. The decode pipeline runs identical code (Packet.decode + generator advancement + to_ndarray) in both passes; optimization targeting this pipeline would reduce cost in both passes proportionally. The individual single-pass decode (96.36s, 35.35%) is the largest single measured stage, and profile evidence corroborates Packet.decode (114.85s self) as the largest single function.

step18_5_acceptance.decoder_call_semantics: preserved unless optimization explicitly targets decode architecture with new parity proof
step18_5_acceptance.optimization_parity_gate: exact canonical signature match required — quality regression is not permitted as a shortcut

evidence_gap_description: RESOLVED. F-0097 gap (unattributed residual of 87.39s, 33.0%) was closed by adding three new non-overlapping wall-clock timers: pass1_decode_or_frame_advance, pass2_decode_or_frame_advance, and pass2_match_and_collect. The residual is now 0.53s (0.19%), confirming all decode work is captured. The primary bottleneck (DECODE_FRAME_PIPELINE) is SAFELY DISCRIMINATED.
f0097_link: F-0097 — RESOLVED by instrumentation: pass1_decode_or_frame_advance + pass2_decode_or_frame_advance + pass2_match_and_collect timers
